fullText: i'm going to show you four incredible open source projects that show you where the world is headed with agents these four projects show the future of how humans are going to interact and use and leverage artificial intelligence the first one is called GStack and it is by Y Combinator President Gary Tan and I just started using it let me show you a few things so just a couple weeks old it is already nearly at 50,000 stars on GitHub and if you don't know who Gary Tan is he is the president of Y Combinator the premier startup accelerator in Silicon Valley gary has invested and overseen thousands of startups including some of the biggest ones you've definitely heard of like Airbnb and Door Dash so what he did was he boiled all of his experience all of his expertise and then combined that with his desire to give solo developers the power of entire development teams and put it in a single package that can be used on top of cloud code codeex or whatever other coding agent you're using this is GStack so he quotes Carpathy at the beginning of the documentation saying "I don't think I've typed a line of code probably since December basically which is an extremely large change." He says "When I heard Karpathy say this I wanted to find out how how does one person ship like a team of 20?" And that is what he's attempting to do here so installing it is very easy down here he gives you this little prompt that you simply copy paste into Claude Code so here's Claude Code for me and as you can see I simply copy pasted it it asked me a few questions asked me for some permissions and then it was done and then in a new session you can type things like slashgstack and then you have a bunch of subtools that you can use with it like slashoff- hours and he goes over what each of these do in the documentation so he describes GStack as a process not a collection of individual tools the purpose is to go through this process to fine-tune your idea fine-tune your market opportunity and then of course build it out and again all of this is based on Gary's incredible amount of experience and expertise building startups so for example he has office hours one of the most well-known parts of the Y Combinator program you go meet with the partners of YC you ask them questions they give you advice but for SLO office hours there are six forcing questions that reframe your product before you write any code again trying to hone in on that idea hone in on the right path forward before writing any of that code here we have plan CEO review which rethink the problem find the 10-star product which is a reference to Airbnb's Brian Chesy who said five stars not enough how do we actually give a 10-star experience as part of the Airbnb experience so that's kind of what he's trying to recreate here we have an engineering manager a senior designer design partner staff engineer debugger so basically what he has done is broken out all of these roles into individual prompts or individual markdown files that help claude code understand how to look at your codebase no longer are you using a single agent for all of these different tasks instead the agent is given much more specific direction in how to look at the codebase through the lens of each of these roles and this is prompt engineering at its finest and the cool thing is it's all open source so you can actually go in and you're essentially able to see how Gary Tan thinks about startups and it's really cool that he open sourced all of it so go in there read all the documentation so this is literally how he thinks about office hours for YC lots of cool features being updated seemingly every day and it's weird because Gary also runs Y Cominator full-time does this part-time he's starting to bring on contributors so if you have some ideas let them know go check this out i'll drop the link in the description below and quick disclosure this segment is sponsored by Microsoft and by the way let me just pause for a second and say nobody actually believes me that this is a full-time job or that this channel is a very successful small business and not only that that I can hire people and produce hundreds of videos per year literally I get asked all the time okay but what's your real job the job of running a small business requires many of the typical tasks digging through docs spreadsheets and notes tasks that I used to do manually but now can be sped up significantly by using AI i went over this in detail recently in a short I did with Microsoft i talk about my journey of becoming a content creator and also how I use Microsoft 365 co-pilot business to help with a lot of the automation so my small team can punch above its weight and so if you want to know how I produce hundreds of videos per year have a number of full-time employees and went from zero to over 500,000 subscribers in about 2 years this is how it's a big part of how my small team can operate as if it were a massive team copilot easily plugs into a lot of the apps that I already use today like Word and Excel it can help me search through all of those documents it can help me do research for videos it could review legal documents so many different things that add up to real productivity gains and business i outlined it all in that short go check it out link in the description now back to the video all right next Hermes Agent by Nouse Research this thing has gone mega viral over the last few days already at 13 12,000 stars again only having come out a few days ago hermes agent is like OpenClaw but apparently it is really good at self-improving this isn't just a coding agent this isn't just an assistant it is much more akin to OpenClaw it is an entire framework an entire operating system that you can use as your personal AI agent so if you're deep in openclaw and you want to explore a great alternative Hermes agent might be really cool for you to check out so let's look at some of the features it comes with a terminal interface a tui with multi-line editing/comand autocomplete conversation history interrupt and redirect and streaming tool output and of course what OpenClaw did and showed the world is go ahead give us the chat apps so it supports Telegram Discord Slack WhatsApp Signal and CLI all from a single gateway very similar to OpenClaw now here is where it is different and I've seen a lot of projects like this come out since Andre Carpathy's auto research project which really gave me so much inspiration to build some of my own self-improving loops but this is a self-improving loop within Hermes agent so a closed learning loop agent curated memory with periodic nudges autonomous skill creation after complex tasks skills self-improve during use so they describe it as the only agent with a built-in learning loop it creates skills from experience improves them during use nudges itself to persist knowledge searches its own past conversations and builds a deepening model of who you are across sessions and of course it has all of the lovely features that OpenClaw comes with like scheduler/cron it can delegate to sub aents and it does that in parallel you can run it anywhere locally in the cloud or anywhere in between it also comes with a nice migration from OpenClaw if you don't want to have to start up and rebuild all of your different workflows all of your memories from scratch which is actually a really nice feature and something probably the industry is going to have to think about how do you test out different agents without starting completely from scratch there probably needs to be a standard created soon all right next is a project the most popular one by far called Superpowers and it is already at 115,000 stars on GitHub it is by author Ora and it basically gives Claude Code superpowers now this is in some ways similar to GStack by Gary Tan but it is much more focused on the development side of things and in one of the funniest descriptions of itself well listen to this your agent puts together an implementation plan that's clear enough for an enthusiastic junior engineer with poor taste no judgment no project context and an aversion to testing to follow that made me chuckle so it emphasizes true TDD which is testdriven development yagy you aren't going to need it and dry these are kind of best practices coding patterns so here's the basic workflow first you brainstorm activates before writing code refinds your rough ideas so very similar to GStack in that way in the YC office hours but now this is kind of just a different flavor of it explores alternatives presents design in sections for validation saves a design document then by default it uses work trees which is really nice for parallelization of coding agents then it writes the plans then it actually executes the plans makes sure it's doing TDD and actually does that code reviews and then finishes the branch now this is a little different in that it comes in the form of a plugin so it's a little bit easier to install than GStack so to install it you simply type claude plugin install superpowers at claude plugins official hit enter and that's it it's done and then as soon as you hit /s superpowers you can start to see all of the different tools available to you so we're going to select /s superpowers brainstorm and it is just going immediately so it has an entire workflow that it's going to go through with me one by one just super useful if you need help thinking through how to start these projects how to actually execute them so in a lot of ways similar to GStack just a very different approach all right and last maybe the most interesting project of the four paperclip this is intended to be an entire company run by AI a zerohuman company already at 33,000 stars now I'm going to give a slight caveat before I get into this i have seen so many different projects claiming to be able to make you money and that is not something I cover often on this channel really ever i need you to know the likelihood of you actually making money from this type of project is low unless you put in the effort and I really want to be transparent about that this is not something where you're just going to click a button say go and all of a sudden you're going to wake up with $1,000 in your bank account it is just never going to happen like that but nonetheless Paperclip is very interesting and I'm seeing more and more of these projects in which a team of agents is supposedly able to run an entire company for you probably the most popular of these which is closed source is a company called Pulsia slight detour Pulsia is created by this guy Ben Sarah AI that runs your company while you sleep so basically you plug it in to your company and it just runs everything for you again not only am I going to give you the warning that most likely you are not going to wake up with money in your bank account just because you submitted a prompt but also I find it difficult to believe that these types of zero human company projects don't have the rough edges and limitations that I truly believe they do so take all of this with a grain of salt all right so what is Paperclip open source orchestration for zero human companies if OpenClaw is an employee Paperclip is the company so Paperclip is a Node.js server and React UI that orchestrates a team of AI agents to run a business bring your own agents assign goals and track your agents work and cost from one dashboard let me show you that dashboard now so this is their demo video let's take a look so design-wise very nice you can see there are issues the engineering team automatically takes those issues starts to build things you can see an org chart right here these are all AI agents your CEO is an agent Claude your CMO is an agent Claude your CTO has two engineers one of which is Claude the other is Codeex you can have different projects that your teams are working on and Paperclip has a lot of really cool features all work is atomic you can track how many tokens you're spending because yes this can get extremely expensive it uses a ticketing system so very similar to if you were on an actual engineering team with humans you would use a ticketing system you would submit issues those issues would get addressed built out and then deployed it does all of that for you and it's all tied to the goals that you set so the agents are always referencing your highlevel goal for the company so if you want to get started it is very easy here's the command paperclipip AI onboard-y and a little bit about their upcoming roadmap they're coming out with a plug-in system like adding a knowledge base custom tracing cues etc you can get openclaw style agents which will just make them more capable and you can chat with them directly through obviously things like telegram you can actually fully encapsulate your company into companies.sh which allows you to export or import an entire organization so there might be a way where you can share your organization with other people although if you're making money why would you do that a skills manager scheduling routines better budgeting and more so very cool project i have not tested this myself yet but I've seen a lot of other people try it and say it's really cool it's just very experimental again don't expect to wake up and make a bunch of money it's just not going to happen but it is a vision into what's coming these agents are getting more capable the harnesses the projects around them are getting more capable and so with that if you're early and you're seeing these things you're experimenting with them you have a huge advantage versus everybody else who are still maybe just using chat GPT for Q&A for example this is the cutting edge of what's happening in artificial intelligence all four of these
